bullets.text:
More than 
A31bn has been pledged for Sudan at a conference in Berlin, exceeding the funding target amidst a deepening humanitarian crisis, though prospects for a ceasefire remain distant.
A former US Marine pilot, Daniel Duggan, has lost his appeal against extradition from Australia. He was arrested in 2022 over allegations of illegally training the Chinese military in South Africa.
Nine people, including eight students and one teacher, were killed in a second Turkish school shooting within two days.
A massive crude oil tanker has docked in Newcastle, replenishing regional New South Wales diesel supplies amid ongoing Middle East unrest.
A fire at the Viva oil refinery in Geelong has been extinguished after 13 hours, with concerns remaining about fuel supply and the full extent of damage.
NSW nurses have secured pay rises of up to 28 per cent, bringing an end to a two-year dispute, with the government upholding the court's decision.
Gulf states, including Abu Dhabi, Qatar, and Kuwait, are turning to private deals for wartime borrowing, avoiding public markets as the Iran conflict impacts their economies.
China's economic growth exceeded forecasts in the first quarter of 2026, suggesting limited immediate impact from the war in Iran on its economy, despite weak consumer spending.
The conflict in Iran is expected to significantly deplete the Gulf's $6 trillion treasure chest, complicating financial management for custodians of Middle Eastern oil fortunes.
